title: Status update on Feature Requests – Q2 2024

posts.author: dipro (Dipro Bhowmik)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have an exciting update about the feature request backlog in our community.

We have managed to put a TON of requests onto our product roadmap!

This is part of a larger campaign to make our product roadmap more transparent for YOU, our developer community.

What’s on the roadmap:

We’re planning to release the following features by the end of the year Things can shift, but this is what we’re planning so far.

  • Querying Formula column values
  • Add item_count to groups
  • Accessing archived Items in a board via monday API
  • Add new pulses at the top of board/group
  • SEO improvements for public marketplace (loading time, proper HTML links, etc)
  • Change pricing for apps using a web UI (not form)
  • ItemCard - delete item & create item feature
  • Trigger: “When an item is deleted”
  • Enable some blocks with dependencies to be published
  • Custom conditions for automations
  • Workflow block ID in app payload

In our long-term roadmap:

These features are in our vision for the platform, but we can’t share a specific ETA yet.

  • Api feature request: expose user.time_format & date_format
  • Rename of column IDs
  • Bulk update of Items through API?
  • Expose board order value on items/subitems
  • Create Multiple items at once
  • Changing/Setting Group ID values
  • Monday storage as a database
  • Affiliate / Referral commission in the monetization SDK
  • Subitems columns in custom integration
  • Lifecycle event when user starts the app demo
  • Applying subscription discounts via the API?
  • Allow app developers to test monetization flow in their app
  • App billing - allow devs to prices for existing users (not just new ones)
  • App bundles - to increase sales
  • Are there plans to add graphQL support for vendors to query things like subscriptions?

Features we’re still researching

These are interesting but require deeper research. We want to invest in them, we are just not sure what the solution will look like.

  • Show warning when app is restricted to specific workspaces & better error messages
  • Custom development profiles with stages/parameters

What’s next?

Building all of this, of course! In a few months, we’ll repeat the process – update statuses and prioritize the new or newly-popular items.

Read this topic for more about our feature request discovery and tagging process.

Disclaimer: While these are estimations and the best information so far, our plans can change given shifting business priorities or team capacity!
